questions.question: What is the largest dinosaur?
questions.fun_fact: The Brachiosaurus could reach up to 85 feet in length.

questions.question: Which dinosaur had three horns?
questions.fun_fact: The Triceratops had a large frill to protect its neck.

questions.question: What dinosaur is known for its plates?
questions.fun_fact: The Stegosaurus had 17 plates along its back.

questions.question: Which dinosaur was a fast runner?
questions.fun_fact: The Velociraptor could run up to 40 mph.

questions.question: What dinosaur had a long neck?
questions.fun_fact: The Brachiosaurus could reach leaves high in trees.

questions.question: Which dinosaur had a clubbed tail?
questions.fun_fact: The Ankylosaurus used its tail as a weapon.

questions.question: What dinosaur could fly?
questions.fun_fact: The Pteranodon had a wingspan of up to 33 feet.

questions.question: Which dinosaur was heavily armored?
questions.fun_fact: The Ankylosaurus had bony plates covering its body.

questions.question: What dinosaur is known for its size?
questions.fun_fact: The T-Rex could weigh up to 9 tons.

questions.question: Which dinosaur had a crest on its head?
questions.fun_fact: The Pteranodon's crest helped with balance.

questions.question: What dinosaur had the strongest bite?
questions.fun_fact: The T-Rex had a bite force of about 12,800 pounds.

questions.question: Which dinosaur had a beak like a parrot?
questions.fun_fact: The Triceratops used its beak to clip plants.

questions.question: What dinosaur had spikes on its tail?
questions.fun_fact: The Stegosaurus could swing its spiked tail for defense.

questions.question: Which dinosaur was likely covered in feathers?
questions.fun_fact: The Velociraptor may have used feathers for warmth or display.

questions.question: What dinosaur had nostrils on top of its head?
questions.fun_fact: The Brachiosaurus's high nostrils may have helped it breathe while eating.

questions.question: Which dinosaur had the thickest skull?
questions.fun_fact: The Pachycephalosaurus had a skull up to 10 inches thick.

questions.question: What dinosaur had the longest claws?
questions.fun_fact: The Therizinosaurus had claws up to 3 feet long.

questions.question: Which dinosaur was the first to be discovered?
questions.fun_fact: The Megalosaurus was the first dinosaur to be scientifically described in 1824.

questions.question: What dinosaur had the most teeth?
questions.fun_fact: The Nigersaurus had over 1,000 teeth in its mouth at once.

questions.question: Which dinosaur had a sail on its back?
questions.fun_fact: The Spinosaurus's sail may have helped regulate body temperature.

questions.question: What dinosaur was named after a plate?
questions.fun_fact: The name Stegosaurus means 'roofed lizard'.

questions.question: Which dinosaur had a frill and three horns?
questions.fun_fact: The Triceratops could weigh up to 12 tons.

questions.question: What dinosaur was known as the 'thunder lizard'?
questions.fun_fact: The Brontosaurus was once thought to be a Brachiosaurus.

questions.question: Which dinosaur had a dome-shaped head?
questions.fun_fact: The Pachycephalosaurus may have headbutted rivals.

questions.question: What dinosaur had a long, thin jaw full of sharp teeth?
questions.fun_fact: The Spinosaurus was likely the largest carnivorous dinosaur.

questions.question: Which dinosaur had armor plates and spikes?
questions.fun_fact: The Ankylosaurus was so well-armored it was like a tank.

questions.question: What dinosaur had a crest that could make sounds?
questions.fun_fact: The Parasaurolophus's crest was like a trombone.

questions.question: Which dinosaur was a good swimmer?
questions.fun_fact: The Spinosaurus had webbed feet for swimming.

questions.question: What dinosaur had a tail club for defense?
questions.fun_fact: The Ankylosaurus's tail club could break bones.

questions.question: Which dinosaur was the fastest?
questions.fun_fact: The Compsognathus was about the size of a chicken.
